skill.name: vue-router-best-practices
skill.description: Guidance for applying Vue Router 4 best practices across navigation guards, route params, lifecycle behavior, and production routing setup.
skill.summary: Review Vue Router code for common guard, params, lifecycle, and routing setup mistakes.
skill.author: github.com/vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found them to be false positives in markdown documentation and Vue Router examples. No executable skill code, prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from instructional code examples only.

low_findings.title: Markdown Formatting Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. The flagged lines are markdown inline code, code fences, route paths, and Vue Router examples, not shell, Ruby, or executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown prose and fenced Vue or JavaScript examples. No file defines executable scripts, shell hooks, package commands, or runtime code for the skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The network alerts are false positives. The URLs are license attribution and reference links to Vue, Vue Router, GitHub RFCs, and Vue School documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain static markdown hyperlinks only. I found no fetch, webhook, telemetry, credential upload, or active network code in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Vue Lazy Loading Examples Misclassified as Script Risk
low_findings.description: The dynamic import alerts are false positives. The imports are standard Vue Router lazy-loaded component examples inside documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import expressions appear only inside markdown examples for route components. They are not executed by the skill and do not load remote or user-controlled modules.

low_findings.title: Hash Routing Text Misclassified as Reconnaissance or Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: The blocker alerts are false positives. The flagged hash references describe URL hash routing and route hash changes, not cryptographic hashing or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is browser routing: URL hashes, hashchange listeners, and Stack Overflow warning text. I found no weak cryptographic algorithm use or host reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Vue Router mistakes can cause stale data, broken guards, and redirect loops. This skill gives focused review guidance for common Vue Router 4 routing patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains when beforeEnter, beforeEach, beforeRouteEnter, and onBeforeRouteUpdate run.
Identifies stale data risks when route params change without remounting components.
Shows safer return-based navigation guard patterns for Vue Router 4.
Covers redirect loop prevention and route meta based guard design.
Compares simple hash routing with Vue Router for production applications.
Provides cleanup guidance for hashchange listeners in simple routing prototypes.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not analyze a repository automatically.
Examples use placeholder functions such as fetchUser and checkAuth.
It focuses on Vue Router 4 and may not fit older router versions.
It does not replace security review of real authentication and authorization code.

use_cases.title: Review Auth Guards
use_cases.description: Check protected routes for missing awaits, deprecated next calls, and redirect loop risks.

use_cases.title: Fix Stale Route Data
use_cases.description: Update components that rely on route params so data refreshes when the same component is reused.

use_cases.title: Plan Production Routing
use_cases.description: Choose Vue Router patterns for lazy loading, nested routes, route meta fields, and browser history behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Basic Route
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small route definition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Vue Router route definition for common best practice issues. Focus on params, lazy loading, and route meta use.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Stale Data
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate fix for reused route components.
prompt_templates.prompt: This Vue component shows stale data when moving between route params. Use the Vue Router best practices skill to explain the cause and suggest fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize Guards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced migration from legacy navigation guard syntax.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor these Vue Router guards from next based syntax to return based syntax. Preserve behavior and call out any async or redirect risks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Routing Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a production single page application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Vue Router setup for production readiness. Include navigation guards, route params, lazy loading, redirect loops, and listener cleanup concerns.

output_examples.input: A guard redirects unauthenticated users to the login route without checking the target route.
output_examples.output: The review flags an infinite redirect risk and recommends excluding public routes or checking route meta before redirecting.

output_examples.input: A user profile page loads data only in the mounted lifecycle hook.
output_examples.output: The review explains component reuse on param changes and recommends watching route params or using an update guard.

output_examples.input: A small prototype uses hashchange listeners for routing.
output_examples.output: The review confirms the pattern is acceptable for prototypes and recommends removing listeners during component unmount.

best_practices: Use return based navigation guards for Vue Router 4 unless maintaining legacy code.
Watch route params or use onBeforeRouteUpdate when a reused component depends on params.
Use route meta fields to centralize authentication and authorization guard logic.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on mounted hooks for route-param-dependent data.
Do not redirect from a guard without excluding the redirect target.
Do not leave hashchange listeners attached after a component unmounts.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Vue code?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with examples and review guidance.

faq.question: Which Vue Router version does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Vue Router 4 patterns used with Vue 3 applications.

faq.question: Can it help migrate from next based guards?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains return based guard syntax and legacy next usage risks.

faq.question: Does it cover route param updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains why reused components need watchers or route update guards.

faq.question: Can it replace an application security audit?
faq.answer: No. It helps with router patterns, but real auth logic still needs project-specific review.

faq.question: Does it recommend Vue Router for every app?
faq.answer: No. It allows simple routing for learning and prototypes, while recommending Vue Router for production apps.
